abraham-lincoln.caption: President Abraham Lincoln with Gen. McClellan and officers at Antietam, October 1862 — Lincoln pressed his cautious general to pursue Lee.
abraham-lincoln.alt: A photograph of President Abraham Lincoln standing in a tent encampment among Union officers at Antietam in 1862.
abraham-lincoln.periodNote: Alexander Gardner photograph at Antietam, October 1862 — a genuine wartime field photograph.
abraham-lincoln.credit: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division, LC-DIG-cwpb-04352 (digital file from original neg.), uncompressed archival TIFF version (20 MiB), color

jefferson-davis.caption: Jefferson Davis, President of the Confederacy — a government its own leaders founded to preserve and extend slavery.
jefferson-davis.alt: A wartime portrait photograph of Jefferson Davis, President of the Confederate States.
jefferson-davis.periodNote: A wartime Brady photographic portrait (1860s) — period-accurate.
jefferson-davis.file: File:President Jefferson Davis, Confederate States of America.jpg

robert-e-lee.caption: Gen. Robert E. Lee, commander of the Army of Northern Virginia, in an 1863 wartime portrait.
robert-e-lee.alt: A wartime portrait photograph of Gen. Robert E. Lee in his Confederate uniform coat.
robert-e-lee.credit: File from The Photographic History of The Civil War in Ten Volumes: Volume Two, Two Years of Grim War. The Review of Reviews Co., New York. 1911. p. 240.

ulysses-s-grant.caption: Lt. Gen. Ulysses S. Grant at his Cold Harbor headquarters, 1864 — the general who pressed the war relentlessly to its end.
ulysses-s-grant.alt: A photograph of Lt. Gen. Ulysses S. Grant standing beside a tree before a tent at his field headquarters at Cold Harbor.
ulysses-s-grant.periodNote: An 1864 field photograph at Cold Harbor, Virginia — period-accurate.
ulysses-s-grant.file: File:Lt. Gen. Ulysses S. Grant standing by a tree in front of a tent, Cold Harbor, Va. - NARA - 524455.jpg
ulysses-s-grant.credit: U.S. National Archives and Records Administration

william-tecumseh-sherman.caption: Maj. Gen. William T. Sherman, whose 1864 campaigns through Georgia and the Carolinas broke the Confederacy's war-making capacity and its will.
william-tecumseh-sherman.alt: A wartime portrait photograph of Maj. Gen. William T. Sherman in uniform.
william-tecumseh-sherman.periodNote: A wartime photographic portrait (Brady studio) — period-accurate.
william-tecumseh-sherman.artist: Unknown authorUnknown author or not provided
william-tecumseh-sherman.credit: U.S. National Archives and Records Administration

thomas-stonewall-jackson.caption: Lt. Gen. Thomas "Stonewall" Jackson, photographed in 1863 shortly before he was mortally wounded by his own men at Chancellorsville.
thomas-stonewall-jackson.alt: A wartime portrait photograph of Lt. Gen. Thomas "Stonewall" Jackson in a Confederate uniform coat.
thomas-stonewall-jackson.periodNote: An 1863 photographic portrait, weeks before Chancellorsville — period-accurate.
thomas-stonewall-jackson.credit: Derivative (crop) of: File:Photograph of General Thomas J. "Stonewall" Jackson - NARA - 526067.tif

james-longstreet.caption: Lt. Gen. James Longstreet, Lee's senior corps commander; after the war he became a Republican and was vilified by Lost Cause writers seeking a scapegoat for Gettysburg.
james-longstreet.alt: A wartime portrait photograph of Lt. Gen. James Longstreet in a Confederate uniform.
james-longstreet.artist: The New Orleans Photographic Gallers, A.A. Turner, phot., 57 Camp st. N.O.
james-longstreet.normalizedFrom: File:James Longstreet CDV.png (PNG source re-encoded to JPG + capped 2000px for repo hygiene; re-fetch via sourceUrl for the original)

george-b-mcclellan.caption: Maj. Gen. George B. McClellan, organizer of the Army of the Potomac, whose caution let Lee escape after Antietam.
george-b-mcclellan.alt: A wartime portrait photograph of Maj. Gen. George B. McClellan in uniform.
george-b-mcclellan.credit: Library of Congress Prints and Photographs Division. Brady-Handy Photograph Collection. https://hdl.loc.gov/loc.pnp/cwpbh.00838. CALL NUMBER: LC-BH82- 1334 <

george-g-meade.caption: Maj. Gen. George G. Meade, who took command of the Army of the Potomac days before defeating Lee at Gettysburg.
george-g-meade.alt: A wartime portrait photograph of Maj. Gen. George G. Meade in uniform.
george-g-meade.credit: Library of Congress Prints and Photographs Division. Brady-Handy Photograph Collection. http://hdl.loc.gov/loc.pnp/cwpbh.01199. CALL NUMBER: LC-BH82- 4430 <P

philip-sheridan.caption: Maj. Gen. Philip H. Sheridan, whose 1864 Shenandoah Valley campaign stripped the Confederacy of one of its last granaries.
philip-sheridan.alt: A wartime portrait photograph of Maj. Gen. Philip H. Sheridan in uniform.
philip-sheridan.credit: Library of Congress Prints and Photographs Division. Brady-Handy Photograph Collection. http://hdl.loc.gov/loc.pnp/cwpbh.01008. CALL NUMBER: LC-BH82- 4012 A &lt

braxton-bragg.caption: Gen. Braxton Bragg, the contentious commander of the Confederate Army of Tennessee.
braxton-bragg.alt: A wartime portrait photograph of Gen. Braxton Bragg in a Confederate uniform.
braxton-bragg.credit: Library of Congress Catalog: https://www.loc.gov/pictures/item/cwp2003004346/PP Original url: http://hdl.loc.gov/loc.pnp/cwpb.07427

david-farragut.caption: Rear Adm. David G. Farragut, who forced Mobile Bay in 1864 — "Damn the torpedoes, full speed ahead."
david-farragut.alt: A wartime portrait photograph of Rear Adm. David G. Farragut in a naval uniform.
david-farragut.credit: Restored version of Image:AdmFarragut.jpg; Library of Congress CALL NUMBER: LC-BH82- 4054 , [P&P];

edwin-m-stanton.caption: Edwin M. Stanton, Lincoln's resolute Secretary of War, who organized the Union's vast war machine.
edwin-m-stanton.alt: A wartime portrait photograph of Edwin M. Stanton in a dark suit.
edwin-m-stanton.artist: Mathew Benjamin Brady / Levin Corbin Handy
edwin-m-stanton.credit: Library of Congress Prints and Photographs Division. Brady-Handy Photograph Collection. http://hdl.loc.gov/loc.pnp/cwpbh.04318. CALL NUMBER: LC-BH832- 578 <P

william-h-seward.caption: William H. Seward, Secretary of State, whose diplomacy kept Britain and France from recognizing the Confederacy.
william-h-seward.alt: A wartime portrait photograph of William H. Seward in a suit.
william-h-seward.file: File:William Seward, Secretary of State, bw photo portrait circa 1860-1865.jpg
william-h-seward.credit: This image is available from the United States Library of Congress's Prints and Photographs division under the digital ID cwpb.04948.This tag does not indicate

alexander-h-stephens.caption: Alexander H. Stephens, Confederate Vice President, whose 1861 "Cornerstone Speech" declared slavery the cornerstone of the new government.
alexander-h-stephens.alt: A wartime portrait photograph of Alexander H. Stephens, a thin man in dark clothing.
alexander-h-stephens.credit: Library of Congress Catalog: https://www.loc.gov/pictures/item/cwp2003001868/PP Original url: http://hdl.loc.gov/loc.pnp/cwpb.04947

mary-edwards-walker.caption: Dr. Mary Edwards Walker, Union army surgeon and the only woman ever awarded the Medal of Honor (1865).
mary-edwards-walker.alt: A full-length portrait photograph of Dr. Mary Edwards Walker in her reform dress.
mary-edwards-walker.periodNote: Elliott & Fry, London, 1872 — a postwar studio portrait of the wartime surgeon.

george-h-thomas.caption: Maj. Gen. George H. Thomas, the "Rock of Chickamauga" — a Virginian who stayed loyal to the Union and broke the Army of Tennessee at Nashville.
george-h-thomas.alt: A wartime portrait photograph of Maj. Gen. George H. Thomas in uniform.
george-h-thomas.credit: This image is available from the United States Library of Congress's Prints and Photographs division under the digital ID cwpbh.01069.This tag does not indicate

patrick-cleburne.caption: Maj. Gen. Patrick Cleburne, the "Stonewall of the West," who in 1864 proposed arming and freeing enslaved men for the Confederacy — and was never promoted again.
patrick-cleburne.alt: A wartime portrait photograph of Maj. Gen. Patrick Cleburne in a Confederate uniform.

clara-barton.caption: Clara Barton, the "Angel of the Battlefield," who carried supplies and nursed the wounded at Antietam and Fredericksburg.
clara-barton.alt: A wartime portrait photograph of Clara Barton, a woman in dark Victorian dress.
clara-barton.file: File:Clara Barton - from portrait taken in Civil War and authorized by her as the one she wished to be remembered by LCCN93513623.jpg
clara-barton.artist: Miscellaneous Items in High Demand, PPOC, Library of Congress
clara-barton.credit: Library of Congress Catalog: https://lccn.loc.gov/93513623 Image download: https://cdn.loc.gov/service/pnp/cph/3c00000/3c08000/3c08500/3c08564v.jpg Original url

dorothea-dix.caption: Dorothea Dix, Superintendent of Army Nurses for the Union, who built the army nursing corps.
dorothea-dix.alt: A portrait photograph of Dorothea Dix, a woman in mid-19th-century dress.
dorothea-dix.periodNote: A c.1850s daguerreotype — the standard likeness of the reformer who led the Union army nurses.
